name: From Salir to Messines passing through Alto do Malhão — circular route
name_original: De Salir até Messines passando pelo Alto do Malhão — percurso circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Salir, nestled in Portugal's Serra do Caldeirão, offer diverse and varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a blend of "barrocal" and uplands, providing a landscape of rolling hills and significant elevation changes. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging ascents and enjoyable descents across this varied topography. The area is characterized by its geological formations, including prominent rocky features like Rocha da Pena, which contribute to the scenic backdrop. Best road cycling routes around Salir The most popular road cycling route is From Salir to Messines passing through Alto do Malhão — circular route, a 43.0 miles (69.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes a significant climb through Alto do Malhão. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bike Café - Germano – Alto do Malhão loop from Salir, a moderate 32.8 miles (52.8 km) path. This loop from Salir offers varied terrain and includes sections around Alto do Malhão. Local road cyclists also love the Alto do Malhão – Quinta do Freixo loop from Benafim, a 14.0 miles (22.6 km) trail leading through the rolling hills of the Serra do Caldeirão, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Road cycling around Salir is defined by its rolling hills, varied elevations, and the unique transition between "barrocal" and upland landscapes. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult climbs. The routes in Salir are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Salir's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Salir?
A: Salir offers a diverse selection of road cycling routes. You can find &lt;strong&gt;over 15 routes&lt;/strong&gt; in the area, catering to various preferences and skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Salir?
A: The region around Salir, nestled in the Serra do Caldeirão, features a captivating blend of 'barrocal' and uplands. Cyclists can expect a landscape of &lt;strong&gt;rolling hills and varied elevations&lt;/strong&gt;, providing both challenging ascents and enjoyable descents. Prominent geological features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt; add to the scenic and dynamic terrain.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels in Salir?
A: Yes, Salir offers routes for various abilities. While the majority are &lt;strong&gt;moderate (13 routes)&lt;/strong&gt;, there are also &lt;strong&gt;4 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, and &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt; for those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are the typical durations for road cycling routes in Salir?
A: Route durations vary significantly. For instance, a challenging route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422459428/from-salir-to-messines-passing-through-alto-do-malhao-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Salir to Messines passing through Alto do Malhão — circular route&lt;/a&gt; can take around 3 hours 28 minutes. Shorter, moderate loops such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32269100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Malhão – Quinta do Freixo loop from Benafim&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes starting from Salir?
A: Yes, many routes around Salir are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422459428/from-salir-to-messines-passing-through-alto-do-malhao-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Salir to Messines passing through Alto do Malhão — circular route&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6875255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Café - Germano – Alto do Malhão loop from Salir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I discover along road cycling routes in Salir?
A: The region boasts several stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/219453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trig Point and Panoramic View at Rocha da Pena&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking vistas, including distant ocean views. Additionally, the ruins of Salir Castle provide a great viewpoint over the green hills of Serra do Caldeirão.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or points of interest near the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. You can find beautiful natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Benémola Stream&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/568312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte Grande de Alte&lt;/a&gt;, a natural spring pool. The Fonte Benémola Nature Reserve is also a stunning area with diverse plant and animal species.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Salir?
A: The road cycling experience in Salir is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.6 stars from over 50 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the blend of 'barrocal' and uplands, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs, particularly around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Malhão&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Salir?
A: Spring is particularly beautiful, with wild orchids, narcissus, and wild peonies flourishing, adding to the scenic beauty. The region generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling outside of the hottest summer months, with varied terrain ensuring enjoyable rides throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest to explore near the routes?
A: Yes, Salir itself has a rich history, with archaeological remains and the ruins of Salir Castle offering a glimpse into the past. The village also lies within a UNESCO aspiring Geopark territory, highlighting its unique geological and cultural significance.

Q: Where can I find parking when starting a road cycling route in Salir?
A: While specific parking areas aren't detailed for each route, Salir is a small village and a common starting point for many tours. You can typically find parking within the village or at designated areas near the start of popular routes, allowing easy access to the trails.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through or near the Alto do Malhão?
A: Yes, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto do Malhão&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent feature in many Salir road cycling routes, known for its significant climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422459428/from-salir-to-messines-passing-through-alto-do-malhao-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Salir to Messines passing through Alto do Malhão — circular route&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6875255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Café - Germano – Alto do Malhão loop from Salir&lt;/a&gt; incorporate this challenging and rewarding section.
tips: This is a bike café dedicated exclusively to cyclists, in Alte (Loulé). It is a mandatory meeting point for cyclists traveling the roads and paths of the Algarve coast, as well as a stop to rest and sample typical cuisine.
